an10.48:0.3: Ten Regular Reflections for a Renunciate
an10.48:1.1: "Bhikkhus, there are these ten things that one who has gone forth should often reflect upon. What ten?
an10.48:1.2: (1) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘I have entered upon a classless condition.’
an10.48:1.3: (2) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘My living is dependent upon others.’
an10.48:1.4: (3) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘My deportment should be different.’
an10.48:1.5: (4) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘Do I reproach myself in regard to virtuous behavior?’
an10.48:1.6: (5) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘Do my wise fellow monks, having investigated, reproach me in regard to virtuous behavior?’
an10.48:1.7: (6) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘I must be parted and separated from everyone and everything dear and agreeable to me.’
an10.48:1.8: (7) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘I am the owner of my kamma, the heir of my kamma; I have kamma as my origin, kamma as my relative, kamma as my resort; I will be the heir of whatever kamma, good or bad, that I do.’
an10.48:1.9: (8) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘How am I spending my nights and days?’
an10.48:1.10: (9) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘Do I take delight in empty huts?’
an10.48:1.11: (10) "One who has gone forth should often reflect: ‘Have I attained any superhuman distinction in knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones, so that in my last days, when I am questioned by my fellow monks, I will not be embarrassed?’
an10.48:1.12: "These, bhikkhus, are the ten things that one who has gone forth should often reflect upon."
